seed_instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days.
org_constraint: The email must include at least two reasons for requesting the extension.
new_constraint: Do not include any reasons for requesting the extension.
instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days. The email must include at least two reasons for requesting the extension. Do not include any reasons for requesting the extension.
response:
Here are a few options, depending on the tone you want to convey (formal, slightly more casual but still professional).

**Option 1: Formal and Direct**

Subject: Extension Request - [Your Name] - [Course Name] Project

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

I am writing to respectfully request a short extension on the [Project Name] assignment, which is currently due on [Date, e.g., Wednesday, October 25th].

I have been working diligently on the project, but I am finding that I need a few extra days to ensure the quality of my submission meets the standards we have discussed in class. I would be very grateful if I could have an extension until [Suggest New Date, e.g., Friday, October 27th].

Thank you for considering my request. I apologize for any inconvenience this may cause.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
[Your Student ID]

***

**Option 2: Slightly More Apologetic Tone**

Subject: Request for Extension - [Course Name] Project

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

I hope this email finds you well.

I am writing to ask if it would be possible to receive a brief extension for the [Project Name] project, which is due in two days.

I am committed to turning in my best work, and I believe a few extra days would allow me to properly finalize and polish the assignment. Would it be possible to submit it by [Suggest New Date]?

Thank you very much for your understanding and flexibility regarding this deadline.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
[Your Student ID]<turn|>
